operating_state: Net sales reached $11.5 billion in Q3 2021, up 29% from prior year, with nine-month revenue of $32.7 billion representing 27% growth driven by strong demand across all segments. Production and precision agriculture, small agriculture and turf, and construction and forestry all delivered robust double-digit growth supported by broad-based global strength. Financial services portfolio expanded to $31.4 billion from $27.8 billion, reflecting increased customer financing activity amid robust equipment demand.
cost_structure: Gross margin expanded significantly as cost of sales ratio improved to 72.7% in Q3 from 74.2% prior year, driven by favorable pricing and higher volumes offsetting material cost inflation. Operating profit increased 70% in Q3 and 119% for nine months despite higher production costs and supply chain pressures. Inventory levels increased reflecting supply chain positioning and production scaling.
material_events: The company acquired Bear Flag Robotics for $250 million in August to accelerate autonomous tractor technology and entered joint venture dissolution with Hitachi for $275 million to acquire full ownership of excavator manufacturing entities. Share repurchases totaled $1.8 billion during nine months while quarterly dividend increased to $1.05 per share, representing 17% growth. The company recorded asset impairments primarily for German operations.
macro_exposures: Supply chain pressures and critical parts shortages persist with material cost inflation affecting production schedules and margins. Foreign currency derivative notional amounts increased with currency translation providing positive impact for nine months. COVID-related payment relief represents approximately 3% of financing receivables portfolio while trade restrictions and commodity price volatility present ongoing operational challenges.
forward_outlook: Industry sales forecasts show large agricultural machinery up 25% in U.S. and Canada, European agricultural machinery up 10-15%, and construction equipment up 15-20% for 2021. The Hitachi joint venture dissolution expected to close in first half of fiscal 2022 subject to regulatory approvals. Management delayed planned voluntary OPEB contribution from Q4 2021 to fiscal 2022.
uncertainty_profile: Unrecognized tax benefits totaled $772 million with significant amounts potentially affecting effective tax rate if recognized. The company adopted new credit loss accounting standards requiring expected loss methodology. Ongoing supply chain disruptions, commodity price volatility, and COVID pandemic duration uncertainties present operational and margin risks for future periods.
